campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships in the city. They also discussed extending an emergency ordinance and moving forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 for youth recreation scholarships
Emergency ordinance extension being considered
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application submission for bridge project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide how to distribute grant money to community organizations and events. They approved funding for specific groups that applied for grants, set aside money for a competitive grant program, and made some clarifications to the rules for how community events can apply for money and close streets.
keyTopics: Approved specific organizations receiving grant funding and the dollar amounts each will get
Set funding amount for the Competitive Event Grant Program for the upcoming fiscal year
Updated the rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grants to make them clearer

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026, to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council reviewed reports from various committees and discussed upcoming meetings for city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Scheduling of board and commission meetings
Documents available at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale. The group approved meeting notes from their previous session and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support local housing and human services.
keyTopics: Federal grant applications for affordable housing projects
Community development funding decisions
Review of February commission meeting minutes
Local nonprofits and organizations seeking funding

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on ways to support local businesses and encourage shopping in Cupertino. They discussed a program to help businesses that may not have proper licenses get into compliance, plans to promote local shopping through a summer campaign, and new incentives to attract and help retail stores, small businesses, and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help non-compliant businesses
Outreach efforts at shopping centers to connect with local business owners
Summer of Celebrations campaign to encourage residents to shop locally
Public-private partnerships for city event sponsorships
Economic incentives for retail, small businesses, and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Historical and Landmarks Commission is holding a hearing on April 2, 2026 that you can attend in person or join remotely via Zoom. If you want to speak up about historical or landmark issues affecting the city, you can submit written comments by email up until noon on the day of the meeting.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment deadline: noon on April 2
Zoom access: Meeting ID 972 3326 2035

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to recognize Women's History Month and approve two main agreements. The council agreed to let the American Red Cross use city facilities during emergencies, and approved spending about $93,400 per year for three years on email security software (Mimecast) to protect the city's computers and data.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($280,000+ total cost)
